title: euphrasia nemorosa subsp. ericetorum
intro: Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. ericetorum, also known as heath eyebright, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly found in mountainous regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an eye wash and an anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids. The health benefits of Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. ericetorum include its ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reduce redness, and alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and eye drops, which can be used to treat a range of eye-related issues.
